description: Combretum aculeatum is a species of flowering plant in the bushwillow genus Combretum, family Combretaceae. It is native to the Sahelian and Sudanian savannas and adjacent forest–savanna mosaic in Africa and Saudi Arabia, and has been introduced to Myanmar. High in protein, it is greatly relished as a browse by wild and domesticated mammalian herbivores, except elephants.
origin: MLI, SUD OO, NGR OO, WSA OO, NORTHERN AFRICA
synonyms: ["Combretum leuconili", "Seguiera nudiflora", "Combretum aculeatum f. kotschyanum", "Commiphora holstii", "Combretum denhardtiorum", "Combretum alternifolium", "Combretum aculeatum f. stenophyllum", "Combretum aculeatum f. schimperi", "Poivrea nudiflora", "Combretum aculeatum f. nudiflorum"]

care.tips: Native to: SUD, ARABIAN PENINSULA, GHA OO, ERI, GHA

description:
Combretum farinosum is a species of bushwillow in the genus Combretum, native to Central and South America. The species was first described Carl Sigismund Kunth.
The plant is widely used as perfume ingredient in cosmetics.

description: Combretum glutinosum is a shrub species of the genus Combretum, found in the Sahel belt in parts of Senegal, Burkina Faso, Ghana, Mali, the Gambia, Niger, Nigeria and Cameroon, across to parts of Sudan. It is known as dooki in Pulaar, Kantakara in Hausa, rat in Wolof, jambakatan kè in Mandinka, and yay in Serer. Its synonyms are Combretum cordofanum Engl. & Diels, C. passargei Engl. & Diels, C. leonense Engl. & Diels.
origin: GUI, SIE, SEN, MLI OO, CMN OO
synonyms: ["Combretum glutinosum var. relictum", "Combretum tisserantii", "Combretum oubanguense", "Combretum glutinosum var. passargei", "Combretum schweinfurthii", "Combretum leonense", "Combretum passargei", "Combretum relictum", "Combretum cordofanum"]

description: Combretum hereroense, commonly known as the russet bushwillow and the mouse-eared combretum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is found from eastern Africa to northern South Africa. Over its extensive range it is variable with respect to leaf shape, fruit size and indumentum.
origin: ETH, TVL NW, SUD OO, SWZ, NAM OO

description: Combretum micranthum is a species of flowering plant in the family Combretaceae. It is a shrub known as kinkeliba in Guinea, Benin, Burkina Faso, Senegal, Mali and the Gambia across multiple regional dialects, where it is often found in tiger bush and on hills in West Africa. It is used for making tea and has uses in traditional medicine, and is used by many West African Muslims during Ramadan to break their fast. The name kinkeliba is believed to come from the Fulani language.
origin: MLI, GUI, SIE OO, NGR OO, GHA

care.tips: Native to: BEN OO, COM MA, SIE OO, BKN OO, BEN

description: Combretum zeyheri, the large-fruited bushwillow or Zeyher's bushwillow, is a species of flowering plant in the family Combretaceae, usually found growing on acidic or sandy soils in tropical African savannas. A small to medium-sized tree, its roots are used as a source of material for making baskets and as a traditional medicine for haemorrhoids.

description: Guiera is a flowering plant genus in the family Combretaceae. Guiera senegalensis is the only known species in the genus, found in Tropical Africa in dry areas from Senegal to Sudan. The plant produces the tannin 3,4,5-Tri-O-galloylquinic acid and several alkaloids of the harmane family.
origin: GUI, SEN, MLI OO, CMN OO, GAM OO

description: Laguncularia is a genus of plants in the family Combretaceae. The only species in the genus is Laguncularia racemosa, the white mangrove.
origin: NIC, SIE OO, MXC TL, CMN, GAM OO
synonyms: ["Schousboea commutata", "Conocarpus racemosus", "Laguncularia racemosa var. glabriflora", "Laguncularia racemosa f. longifolia", "Rhizaeris alba", "Laguncularia obovata", "Horau racemosus", "Laguncularia martii", "Laguncularia glabriflora"]

description: Lumnitzera racemosa, commonly known as the white-flowered black mangrove, is a species of mangrove in the family Combretaceae. It is found on the eastern coast of Africa and other places in the western Indo-Pacific region. It has one accepted variety from the noniminate species which is Lumnitzera racemosa var. lutea (Gaudich.) Exell.
origin: IND KE, QLD QU, NNS, SUL, VAN

description:
Terminalia amazonia is a species of tree in the Combretaceae family. It is native to North America and South America and has been used for commercial logging. The wood is hard and durable.
In Belize, Terminalia amazonia is widely located in the Mountain Pine Ridge.
Terminalia avicennioides is a tree species in the genus Terminalia found in West Africa.

ja: ブラックオリーブツリー

description: Terminalia latipes is a tree of the family Combretaceae native to northern Australia.

Terminalia leiocarpa is a species of tree in the genus Terminalia. It is a deciduous tree native of tropical Africa from Senegal and Guinea in the west to Eritrea in the east and as far south as the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

Terminalia sericea is a species of deciduous tree of the genus Terminalia that is native to southern Africa. Its common names include clusterleaf, silver cluster-leaf or silver terminalia in English, vaalboom in Afrikaans and mususu in Venda.
